how_far: Clears the game (通关): defeats the boss and destroys 100% of its HP (e.g. seed 1 wins at ~step 75.7k, level 34). Measured on fresh held-out seeds (never tuned on): ~28% are won outright (game cleared), most non-wins still reach the boss, mean progress ~0.66, estimated eval score ~0.71. On the visible training seeds it does notably better (~54% wins), but that is partly overfit — the fresh-seed numbers are the honest predictor.
failure_modes: Slow-leveling death spiral: on unlucky upgrade-RNG seeds the level/DPS ramps too slowly, so I stay weak and get chipped down by the boss bullet-hell before scaling (early deaths around levels 3-10).
Timeout at ~90%: on some seeds DPS ramps late and I reach the final boss layer but run out of the 90k-step budget before destroying it.
Enemy/boss body contact is an instant-ish kill (damage ~ enemy HP); fleeing UP into the boss was a major early-death cause (fixed with anti-top positioning + a wide boss margin).
lessons: Enemy BODY contact is the #1 threat (lethal, ~= enemy HP; elites/boss = instant kill) — never touch one; bullets (300/450 dmg) are secondary.
Survival is necessary but LEVELING SPEED is what decides win vs timeout vs death — it gates the DPS ramp needed to destroy the ~tens-of-millions boss HP within the budget.

Dodge core: a receding-horizon sampling planner with a DECAYING collision penalty (imminent hits >> far-future) + AABB prediction; boss bullets are constant-velocity so linear prediction is accurate. This prevents corner-trapping and surprise clips.

Fire-rate (floors ~30ms) + multishot are the dominant DPS levers; exp multipliers + full-screen magnet + aggressive exp collection drive the level snowball. Boss uptime and survival directly conflict (the boss fires from its own x), so chasing uptime gets you killed.
Stay BELOW the boss and central-ish; the bottom EDGE is fine (bullet gaps widen lower down) but the CORNERS are death traps.

Beware overfitting: tuning weights on a fixed seed set looked like ~96% but generalized to ~70% on fresh seeds — only general, physics-grounded changes transfer; per-seed weight tweaks are washed out by deterministic chaos.
